name: Okapi
primary_sources: International Union for the Conservation of Nature and Natural Resources

visual_attributes.morphology: medium-sized giraffid with a long neck and large, flexible ears
distinctive_features: striped markings on legs resembling a zebra
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: 1.5 m tall at the shoulder, 2.5 m body length, 200 to 350 kg weight
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: long neck, large ears, horizontal stripes on legs

domain_specific_visual.OSSICONES: short, less than 15 cm, hair-covered horn-like structures on males
domain_specific_visual.SEXUAL_DIMORPHISM: females are taller, slightly redder, and lack ossicones

functional_attributes.primary_function: herbivore feeding on tree leaves, buds, grasses, ferns, fruits, and fungi

domain_specific_functional.NOCTURNAL_ADAPTATION: large number of rod cells for night vision

relationships.description: The okapi and giraffe are the only living members of the family Giraffidae.
The okapi is endemic to the northeast Democratic Republic of the Congo.

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: northeast Democratic Republic of the Congo, possibly southwest of the Congo River
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: known as the African unicorn, symbol of mystery and discovery
